channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: EverQuest Brings Back The Tempest Festival and EverQuest II's May Server Merge is Complete
item.content:encoded:
EverQuest’s Tempest Festival is back, so it is once again time to praise Karana for the harvest and spread his blessings. Meanwhile, the latest EverQuest II server merge is complete, with Zarrakon now folded into Antonia Bayle.

The Tempest Festival is on from now through May 10th, and you can start by speaking to Rosalind Mischeva in the Plane of Knowledge. Then, you’ll help her out by finding members of her clan as they spread the blessings of Karana. You’ll encounter them as you travel around.

Then, if your server unlocked The Buried Sea, there are additional activities, including digging up seashells to complete a collection, to do for levels 55+ and some, 100+.

EverQuest II has completed its planned May server merge and now, Zarrakon is no more. This server merge was announced last month and there were no free transfers off of the server, so if you were on Zarrakon, you’re not on Antonia Bayle.

Affected players can also check out the FAQ  to orient themselves if they were part of this server merge. So if you have some housekeeping to do, like renaming your character or making sure everything came over as intended, you can handle that now.

The Darkpaw team says that this server merge was intended to free up some resources necessary to welcome the new server later in the year.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/everquest-brings-back-the-tempest-festival-and-everquest-iis-may-server-merge-is-complete-2000137991
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: CCP Games Rebrands to Fenris Creations, Focused on 'Long-Term Future of the EVE Universe'
item.content:encoded:
With last week’s announcement that Pearl Abyss had sold CCP Games back to the studio’s leadership team, CCP became independent once more. Today, they announced a rebranding to Fenris Creations, a name tied to the company’s history and values. They’ve also announced a new partnership with Google DeepMind.

The name Fenris Creations is drawn from Norse mythology and something core to the company’s values: 

“When we sought out to make games in 1997 we were grounded by a guiding principle; for a virtual world to have meaning, it must be shaped by risk, loss, renewal, and the people who inhabit it. In short: death is a serious matter.”

With this new era Fenris Creations is “an independent company built to support the long-term future of the EVE universe”, and those big plans continue. This is a transition but not one with layoffs or team changes, the announcement assures us. The EVE Online, EVE Frontier, EVE Vanguard, and EVE Galaxy Conquest teams remain and their studios continue operations. 

The new partnership with Google DeepMind is a research partnership that is “focused on intelligence in complex, dynamic, player-driven systems”, and the draw here was the fact that EVE  already operates like a complex living world. Alexandre Moufarek, Google DeepMind’s director, says “It is a one-of-a-kind simulation for testing general-purpose artificial intelligence in a safe sandbox environment. Their partnership looks to “push the frontier of artificial intelligence and explore new player experiences”.

All of this comes as EVE Online had real momentum heading into 2026, with one of the strongest quarters in the game’s history. With the focus on the long-term, it makes sense coming from this company in particular. Many companies will talk about wanting long-term success without having a 20-year old game successfully running and even breaking records. There is still capacity for surprise and that is also important here.

“New Eden is alive because you keep making it alive”, the announcement says. It will take us all a little time to get used to saying Fenris Creations, but this new chapter is here, to chew over before Fanfest.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/ccp-games-rebrands-to-fenris-creations-focused-on-long-term-future-of-the-eve-universe-2000137990
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
